business.headline: Good Friday 2026: Is stock market open or closed today?Both stock exchanges, the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and the National Stock Exchange (NSE), are closed on April 3 on account of Good Friday.

Form 15G, 15H gone: Will new Form 121 simplify TDS for taxpayers?Form 121 is a self-declaration that allows individuals to request that no TDS be deducted on certain types of income. This usually includes interest earned from bank deposits, post office schemes, or similar sources.

Will the new Income Tax website make filing returns simpler for you?The new website brings together all income tax-related information in one place. Gold, silver prices today: Why is silver falling more than gold?On the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX), gold futures were trading at Rs 1,49,283 at 11:15 am, down Rs 4,425 or 2.88%. Silver futures saw a steeper fall. Prices were at Rs 2,28,891, down Rs 14,610 or 6.00%.

Live: Iran claims second F-35 kill amid war with US, says pilot survival unlikelyUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Iran-US-Israel conflict is intensifying, with tensions spreading across the region and new flashpoints emerging. Iran says the war will continue until its enemies face "humiliation" and "surrender," warning against any US ground invasion, while also claiming unverified strikes on the USS Abraham Lincoln and US drone units at Ali Al Salem Air Base. US President Donald Trump said Iran's largest bridge had been hit and urged Tehran to reach a deal "before it is too late," as US and Israeli attacks intensified, targeting sites in Tehran and key infrastructure. Despite Trump's claim that Iran sought a ceasefire, Tehran has denied it, with the IRGC adding that the Strait of Hormuz will remain closed to its enemies. Iran says more than 2,000 people have been killed and over 26,000 wounded since February 28. Stay with IndiaToday.in for the latest and live updates.

Pete Hegseth asks US Army chief Randy George to step down, retire immediatelyUS Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth has asked Army Chief of Staff Randy George to step down and retire immediately. The reason for the move remains unclear, but it comes amid a broader reshuffle of top military leadership.
